item.title: Africa’s richest man Dangote plans London listing of cement empire
item.description: Billionaire behind $13bn company says rule changes to encourage UK listings make plan more attractive

item.title: Wall Street rebound driven by smallest number of stocks on record
item.description: Soaring S&P propelled by Big Tech equities prompts warnings about ‘fragility’ of rally

item.title: Let’s stop blaming retail investors for wonky markets
item.description: They are finance’s saviours as well as its villains

item.title: Who will drive the driverless car revolution?
item.description: US companies still lead in innovation but their Chinese rivals are speeding ahead in the deployment race

item.title: Citi disappoints investors with modest profit targets
item.description: Shares in Wall Street bank fall 3% in pre-market trading

item.title: Lloyd’s of London debates disclosing findings of probe into governance concerns
item.description: Insurance market hired Freshfields to investigate promotion of executive close to then-CEO

item.title: Can AI help developing countries to ‘leapfrog’ into the future?
item.description: For Africa, opportunities may lie in sequencing

item.title: The mysterious $53bn ‘other income’ boost to AI hyperscaler earnings
item.description: Quantum entanglement

item.title: McDonald’s sales rise as chain reaps rewards for cheap meals push
item.description: Fast food group beat expectations but record US ground beef prices threaten margins

item.title: Private equity tech investor Hg marks down holdings after software sell-off
item.description: Listed vehicle Hg Capital Trust cut valuations of 14 of its 20 largest portfolio companies

item.title: Can Europe close the AI gap with the US and China?
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: a new company seeks to tackle the power constraints on European data centre growth

item.title: ‘BT is back’ as telecoms group revives its consumer brand
item.description: FTSE 100 group launches push to capitalise on its ‘heritage’ after years of prioritising EE and Plusnet

item.title: FirstFT: US fuel exports surge as pump prices rise
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: virus-hit ship sails for the Canary Islands, and Wall Street’s salacious lawsuit

item.title: How the oilman’s president boosted a green transition
item.description: Donald Trump’s Iran war has made fossil fuels expensive and unreliable

item.title: Attenborough at 100: ‘the most consequential broadcaster of our times’
item.description: Career of wildlife filmmaking pioneer has mirrored the evolution of TV itself

item.title: 15 gadgets for fans of an analogue world...
item.description: …who also enjoy their privacy

item.title: UK and US launch disease control measures over hantavirus
item.description: Some passengers had already left cruise ship struck with rodent-borne illness before alarm was raised

item.title: Shell’s profits jump as Iran war delivers windfall
item.description: Energy major warns of lower gas production after damage to its facilities in the Gulf

item.title: Rheinmetall to start producing cruise missiles as early as this year
item.description: Joint venture with Dutch start-up Destinus will manufacture ‘deep strike’ weapons in race to fill European defence gaps

item.title: JD Sports warns of fall in profits as shoppers tighten belts
item.description: UK retailer is trying to steady itself after exit of chair following a boardroom clash

item.title: UniCredit to sell parts of its Russian business
item.description: Bank says move will hit profits by up to €3.3bn

item.title: Maersk warns economic impact of Iran conflict will ripple for months
item.description: World’s second-largest container shipping line says costs have increased by $500mn per month

item.title: Emirates soars to record profit despite Iran war grounding planes
item.description: Dubai airline says it has restored most flights after conflict closed airspace and sent jet fuel prices soaring

item.title: Trading the trends in GDP
item.description: Plus, yen intervention

item.title: SoftBank surges as investors rush back into tech stocks
item.description: Gains for OpenAI and Arm investor help Nikkei 225 to record high after holiday closure

item.title: EU dithers on US trade deal implementation amid Trump threats
item.description: Also in this newsletter: New rail ticket rules, and how to spend Europe’s defence boom

item.title: Samsung workers demand bigger slice of surging AI profits
item.description: South Korean unions threaten strike action in battle for big bonuses and higher wages

item.title: Accelerating Business
item.description: A monthly series that examines how the legal ecosystem uses new technologies to serve fast-changing business needs. First burst for 2026: the mid-career switch from law firm to legal-tech start-up

item.title: Barclays and Citi bosses recommended for honours in King’s birthday list
item.description: CS Venkatakrishnan and Jane Fraser have been proposed alongside a raft of senior current and past politicians

item.title: Marc Rowan rips private capital’s marks
item.description: Apollo Global chief executive warns about ‘day one mark-ups’ of private equity funds sold to retail investors
